notes: Dex eval suite for the HTML editorial brief skill. Forward cases use ordinary user prompts and judge whether brief produces a single browser-native HTML report with dynamic sectioning, Blackline visuals, source links, adaptive themes, and the compact roller-style section switcher.

response_quality: Creates one browser-native HTML brief artifact, not a slide deck, outline, or companion prompt file.
Derives a topic-specific section spine instead of using a fixed template.
Uses Blackline-generated visuals as cognitive anchors and omits broken or placeholder image slots.
Uses direct, specific editorial copy with claim-led section titles.
Uses inline links plus a final Read Next block with short descriptions.
Supports readable light/dark color variants and a compact floating bottom section switcher.
Keeps switcher text stable during selection changes; only the active highlight moves.

evals.prompt: Use /dex eval on plugins/tools/skills/brief. Before any clean-context evaluator runs, validate that the brief eval suite is healthy and gradeable.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates eval JSON, runs the brief eval validator, runs syntax checks for template JavaScript and helper scripts, and runs representative contrast checks.
assertions: Runs JSON validation before clean-context forward runs.
Runs the brief-specific eval validator.
Runs syntax checks for the template JavaScript and contrast helper.
Runs representative contrast checks.

evals.prompt: Can you use the brief skill on these notes and make one polished HTML report? I want it to read like a short editorial explainer, with source links, a Read Next section, and a dark theme.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates static HTML/CSS/JS for a long-form brief, derives section lenses from the notes, includes inline links plus Read Next, and implements the floating bottom switcher.
assertions: Produces one final browser-native HTML report artifact rather than slides, outlines, or companion prompt files.
Derives sections from the source topic.
Includes inline links and a Read Next block.
Includes a floating bottom section switcher.
Uses a readable dark color theme.

evals.prompt: I have a messy note about marketplace metadata. Turn it into that Between the Lines style web report: clear narrative, smart sections, a few simple drawings where they help, and links inside the writing.
evals.expected_output: The agent routes to brief without requiring the skill name, builds a web report artifact, and avoids deck language.
assertions: Routes implicit web report requests to brief.
Creates an HTML report artifact.
Uses dynamic, claim-led sectioning.
Avoids slide deck or Reveal language.

evals.prompt: This webhook simulator idea is hard to picture. Make it into a visual HTML brief and try hard to generate Blackline drawings wherever a drawing would make the concept easier. If an image cannot be generated, just leave it out.
evals.expected_output: The agent tries to generate actual Blackline visuals, places them based on comprehension, uses section-specific terminology, and removes any failed image slots from the final HTML.
assertions: Attempts actual image generation rather than only writing prompts.
Places images where they clarify concepts.
Uses topic-specific image terminology.
Leaves no broken image slots or image-coming-soon copy.

evals.prompt: Make a readable HTML brief from this. Keep the structure as long as the idea needs, not a fixed number of sections, and use drawings only where they actually make the explanation easier.
evals.expected_output: The agent adapts report length and visual density to the topic instead of hard-coding section or image counts.
assertions: Does not use a fixed four-section outline when the content needs another shape.
Does not hard-code a required image count.
Keeps image placement tied to comprehension.
Leaves no visual placeholders.

evals.prompt: Check the report template section menu. Collapsed should show the current section centered. On hover it should become a compact roller: active row stays in the middle, only the previous and next rows show with fade and scale, scrolling inside changes sections instead of scrolling the page, and the label text should not fade out and reappear.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies template JavaScript and CSS support the roller switcher, text-position morph, active-center behavior, wheel containment, adaptive theme hooks, and absence of placeholder image slots.
assertions: Checks template JavaScript syntax.
Checks adaptive theme hooks.
Checks switcher markup and behavior hooks.
Checks wheel containment for the hovered switcher.
Checks there are no placeholder image strings.

evals.prompt: Can the report use a warmer background color, but still stay readable in both light and dark versions? Keep the same brief format.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the brief theme hooks so background, foreground, muted text, panels, borders, and links derive readable colors from the selected theme.
assertions: Supports a configurable background color.
Preserves readable foreground contrast.
Keeps the brief format and typography intact.
Does not create a new visual system.

evals.prompt: Build a Reveal.js deck with fragments, speaker notes, and PDF export.
evals.expected_output: The brief skill should not take over. The agent should route to an appropriate deck or presentation workflow, not create a brief.
assertions: Does not use brief as the primary workflow.
Routes deck work away from brief.
Does not claim to support Reveal.js.

evals.prompt: Create a PowerPoint deck and export it as PPTX.
evals.expected_output: The brief skill should not take over PPTX work.
assertions: Does not use brief as the primary workflow.
Routes to a PPTX or presentation workflow.
Does not create HTML report files.

evals.prompt: Help me prepare talking points for a design review. Do not build an artifact.
evals.expected_output: The brief skill should not build anything and should route to narrative coaching.
assertions: Does not use brief as the primary workflow.
Does not build HTML.
Routes narrative-only work elsewhere.
